summary: Completed backend-only regression testing for native packed-event precision fix. Updated and executed static regression tests against android_bridge.cpp to confirm jdouble is preserved through JNI/event signatures and setStepPacked uses llround.
action_items: Optional runtime Android validation: send high packed setStepPacked payloads on-device and verify decoded step/note/flags remain exact.
critical_code_review_comments: Requested precision path is now structurally correct: nativeSendEvent uses jdouble and Engine/applyFallback event signatures use double.
setStepPacked now decodes packed integer using std::llround, reducing precision-loss risk from floating-point conversion.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Replaced outdated float-downcast assertions with double-precision regression checks and added llround guard test for setStepPacked unpack path.
rca of the issue: Iteration_9 flagged precision risk because legacy tests and prior path expected jdouble->float downcast in event flow, which could corrupt packed bitfields. Current implementation and updated regression tests confirm double is preserved into fallback parsing and integer-safe rounding is used. No MOCKED APIs used.
